kingdoms.research.sci_root.desc: A foundation for richer mines and better stonework.

kingdoms.research.sci_mine_2.desc: Ore veins are easier to trace and exploit.
kingdoms.research.sci_mine_3.desc: Deeper tunnels improve sustained mining speed.
kingdoms.research.sci_mine_cap.desc: The faction learns under-mountain smelting methods.
kingdoms.research.sci_drill_1.desc: Outpost drills are tuned for better output.
kingdoms.research.sci_drill_2.desc: Drill cycles are shortened by improved gearing.
kingdoms.research.sci_drill_cap.desc: A capstone drill protocol focused on interval speed.
kingdoms.research.sci_smelt.desc: Shared furnace practice improves smelting work.
kingdoms.research.sci_lab.desc: Scholars turn discoveries into more science influence.
kingdoms.research.sci_academy.desc: A permanent academy expands research and logistics capacity.
kingdoms.research.eco_root.desc: The faction organizes merchants and buyback ledgers.
kingdoms.research.eco_trade_1.desc: Traders find better value in common resources.
kingdoms.research.eco_trade_cap.desc: The guild dominates local markets for economic influence.
kingdoms.research.eco_land_1.desc: Surveyors reduce the cost of claiming territory.
kingdoms.research.eco_land_2.desc: Land records make further expansion cheaper.
kingdoms.research.eco_land_3.desc: Organized settlers increase available chunk capacity.
kingdoms.research.eco_land_cap.desc: A capstone doctrine for wide territorial growth.
kingdoms.research.eco_tax.desc: Faction holdings begin feeding the treasury.
kingdoms.research.eco_bank.desc: Markets convert trade into more economy influence.
kingdoms.research.eco_vault.desc: Treasury systems become more reliable and profitable.
kingdoms.research.eco_treasury.desc: The faction centralizes wealth and economic influence.
kingdoms.research.war_root.desc: The faction sets up early warning for raids.
kingdoms.research.war_def_1.desc: Warning towers give defenders more time.
kingdoms.research.war_def_2.desc: A trained militia reduces pressure from raiders.
kingdoms.research.war_def_cap.desc: Permanent defenders harden the faction heartland.
kingdoms.research.war_wall_cap.desc: A capstone fortress doctrine for claimed land.
kingdoms.research.war_off_1.desc: Warriors learn to hit harder in faction battles.
kingdoms.research.war_off_2.desc: Kills during war become more strategically valuable.
kingdoms.research.war_off_cap.desc: A capstone command path for military influence.
kingdoms.research.sci_chunk.desc: More chunks can stay force-loaded (+5 per level).
kingdoms.research.sci_ore.desc: Chance for extra ore drops when mining.
kingdoms.research.sci_enchant.desc: Chance to roll an enchantment one level higher.
kingdoms.research.sci_drill_out_2.desc: Even more yield per drill cycle.
kingdoms.research.sci_craft.desc: Chance to craft an extra item.
kingdoms.research.eco_buy_1.desc: The buyer pays more for your goods.
kingdoms.research.eco_buy_2.desc: Traders find more value in resources.
kingdoms.research.eco_steal_1.desc: Raiders steal less gold on defeat.
kingdoms.research.eco_buy_3.desc: Bulk buying is even more profitable.
kingdoms.research.eco_outpost.desc: Discount on outposts and drills from the trader.
kingdoms.research.eco_steal_2.desc: Even less gold lost in a raid.
kingdoms.research.eco_chunk.desc: More chunks can stay force-loaded (+5 per level).
kingdoms.research.eco_steal_3.desc: Maximum protection of the treasury from raids.
kingdoms.research.eco_vill_disc.desc: Discount when trading with ordinary villagers.
kingdoms.research.eco_vill_extra.desc: Chance for an extra item when buying from villagers.
kingdoms.research.war_warn_1.desc: Early warning of an approaching raid.
kingdoms.research.war_reward_1.desc: More reward for a repelled raid.
kingdoms.research.war_fewer.desc: Fewer raiders in an attack wave.
kingdoms.research.war_mob_2.desc: Even more damage to hostile mobs.
kingdoms.research.war_armor.desc: A small armor bonus for members.
kingdoms.research.war_chunk.desc: More chunks can stay force-loaded (+5 per level).
kingdoms.research.war_tnt.desc: Claimed blocks resist explosions during war.
kingdoms.research.war_warn_2.desc: Even more time to prepare for a raid.
kingdoms.research.war_reward_2.desc: Maximum reward for a repelled raid.
kingdoms.research.effect.smelt_speed.det: lit furnaces in claims work about 2x faster
kingdoms.research.effect.enchant_boost.d: 50% chance to raise each applied enchantment by 1 level

kingdoms.guide.body.commands:
Player-command availability depends on faction role and server permissions. [OP] marks operator commands.

Player commands:
- /f - info about your faction.
- /f create <name> - create a faction.
- /f invite <player> - invite a player.
- /f join [faction] - accept an invite.
- /f decline [faction] - decline an invite.
- /f leave - leave your faction.
- /f disband - disband the faction.
- /f rename <name> - rename the faction.
- /f kick <player> - kick a member.
- /f rank|role <player> member|officer - change role.
- /f transfer <player> - transfer leadership.
- /f claim - claim the current chunk.
- /f unclaim - release the current chunk.
- /f pvp on|off - faction PvP.
- /f deposit <amount> - deposit spurs into the treasury.
- /f withdraw <amount> - withdraw spurs from the treasury.
- /f chest add|remove <player> | clear - container access.
- /f info [faction] - faction details.
- /f map [radius] - chunk map in chat.
- /f forceload - toggle loading for the current chunk.
- /f war declare <faction> | end | surrender | status - war.
- /duel <player> - challenge to a duel.
- /duel accept [player] - accept a duel.
- /duel decline <player> - decline a duel.

Operator commands [OP]:
- /kingdoms research complete <node> | all | reset - manage research.
- /kingdoms spawntrader - spawn a Kingdoms Trader.
- /kingdoms plot create <price> | remove <id> | setprice <id> <price> | reclaim <id> | list - market plots.
- /kingdoms sanctuary vulnerable <player> - disable a player's spawn protection until their next death.
- /kingdoms dimension nether|end open|close|status|wipe [cancel] - manage dimensions.
- /kingdoms raid force|warn|outpost <faction> - manage raids.
kingdoms.guide.body.factions:
Factions are player kingdoms.

Found one at a Faction Table: it costs spurs and lets you set a name, colour and emblem, and pick two bonuses.

Roles:
- Leader: full control.
- Officer: manage members, chunks and war.
- Member: build on faction land.

From the table you can invite players, change roles, transfer leadership, configure internal PvP, leave, or disband the faction.
kingdoms.guide.body.territory:
Use the Faction Table map or Xaero's map to claim chunks around your territory.

Each claimed chunk costs spurs, and new chunks must border your existing territory.

On another faction's land you cannot break or place blocks, use machinery, or open containers. Separate rules apply during war.

The Management Key sets container access: Personal, Faction, Public or Whitelist. Use /f forceload or Xaero's map menu to keep a selected chunk loaded within your faction's slot limit.
kingdoms.guide.body.influence:
Influence comes in three kinds: Science, Economy and War.

- Science: craft modded items.
- Economy: trade with villagers, sell to buyers, and (for Merchants) earn passive treasury income.
- War: kill hostile mobs and enemy players, and win wars.

Turning in crystals at the table grants influence of their type. Influence is spent on research in the matching branch; claiming chunks costs spurs.
kingdoms.guide.body.research:
Research is a tree at the Faction Table, split into Science, Economy and War branches.

Nodes cost influence and take real time to finish. They grant bonuses such as faster mining, cheaper claims, extra force-load slots, or claims that resist TNT.

Only an officer or the leader can start research.
kingdoms.guide.body.bonuses:
Pick two bonuses when founding a faction:

- Miners: extra ore and faster mining.
- Farmers: extra crops and a chance for twin animals.
- Builders: cheaper chunk claims and larger outposts.
- Assassins: more damage from behind.
- Hookah Smokers: speed, armour and damage while carrying a hookah.
- Enchanters: combine incompatible and overleveled enchantments.
- Merchants: better seller prices and treasury income.
- Nomads: faster mounts.
- Researchers: research progresses 20% faster.
kingdoms.guide.body.war:
Declare war at the Faction Table: pick a target, then choose a war type and a reason. The war type is informational.

Score points by killing enemies (25) and by destroying enemy-built blocks by hand or explosion (2, capped per minute). The goal is 1000 points; a boss bar shows the score. You may break blocks and place TNT in enemy territory, but containers remain protected.

Win by reaching the goal or by enemy surrender. The victor gains War influence and loot (money and resources) for the lead faction. Territory changes are rolled back after the war.

After a war there is a 48-hour cooldown before you can declare another.
kingdoms.guide.body.alliances:
Propose or accept an alliance at the Faction Table.

Allies may build in each other's claims. If an ally is attacked, you can Join the War on their side - their land becomes a war zone.

Joining the winning side rewards War influence plus a random amount of Economy or Science influence.
kingdoms.guide.body.archive:
The War Archive block stores the history of every finished war: the sides, war type, reason, victor, score and date.

Right-click the War Archive to browse the records.
kingdoms.guide.body.outposts:
Outposts are small remote holdings founded with an Outpost Charter on a core block. Place Drills on the resource clusters inside an outpost to extract resources over time.

Raids: while at least one member is online, a faction may be raided after a 48-hour grace period measured from faction creation. Raiders spawn near your territory; defeat them all within the time limit to win a reward. On defeat, the faction loses edge chunks and part of its treasury. Rogue raiders can also seize an undefended outpost.
kingdoms.guide.body.trade:
Spurs are used for purchases, the faction treasury, and market plots.

The Kingdoms Trader sells the Faction Table, Outpost Charter, Drill, Management Key, and Seller Catalogue. Sellers receive nine random offers each day with per-player sale limits; the Catalogue shows every seller's current stock. Sales award spurs and may grant Economy influence.

The Banker sells a Bank Terminal, Vendor, White Bank Card, Red Identification Card, and Banking Guide.

Market plots are located in the protected spawn zone. Purchases use coins from your inventory and bank account. Owners may build, trust players or factions, resell the plot, or return it to the server; returning a plot restores its original build.
kingdoms.guide.body.world:
The spawn zone is protected from building, PvP, hostile mobs, and explosions. Operators and market-plot owners may build inside the plots they control.

Operators may close the Nether or End. The Dimension Key opens controls for opening or closing either dimension and scheduling it to be wiped on the next server start.
